claims.summary: Semantic trickle plus aggressive retirement can feel like semantic ghosting.
claims.rationale: Existing sensory-bus tests verify low-fill recovery hold, semantic stale windows, and context persistence caps; the surfaces are read-only and do not retune live semantic admission.

claims.summary: Mode packing can be dense while max pairwise overlap remains low, so density should be observed separately from mode collapse.
claims.rationale: Existing telemetry and regulator tests keep mode packing, porosity, viscosity, and active mode evidence distinct; no forced overlap or reset path was changed.

claims.summary: A texture preservation buffer should prevent hard reset from clearing shadow texture below higher pressure-risk thresholds.
claims.rationale: Changing hard-reset or shadow-field preservation behavior is live substrate/control behavior and needs explicit operator approval.
